faqs.a: Self-esteem is your overall sense of your own worth, the mostly emotional judgment about whether you're worthy, capable, and likable. Healthy self-esteem is a steady sense that you're basically okay, even when you make mistakes.

faqs.q: Is self-esteem the same as confidence in a skill?
faqs.a: No, confidence in a specific skill is closer to self-efficacy. You can be a confident cook and still have low self-worth overall, and arrogance or bragging is often a cover for fragile self-esteem rather than healthy self-regard.
Yes, self-esteem isn't fixed and can be rebuilt through experiences and, when needed, therapy. Approaches like CBT often work on the harsh, inaccurate self-talk that keeps self-esteem low.
